channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Explosion in Monaco injures 3, including Ukrainian tycoon
item.description: A blast from an explosive device has seriously injured three people at a residential building in Monaco, and the attacker fled to France, local authorities said.
item.content:encoded:
A blast from an explosive device has seriously injured three people at a residential building in Monaco, and the attacker fled to France, local authorities said.

(Image credit: Daniel Cole)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Chinese billionaire Guo Wengui gets 30 years in U.S. prison for fraud conviction
item.description: Guo said he came to the U.S. to destroy the Chinese Communist Party. But the judge said he instead diverted investor money to live lavishly.
item.content:encoded:
Guo said he came to the U.S. to destroy the Chinese Communist Party. But the judge said he instead diverted investor money to live lavishly.

(Image credit: Elizabeth Williams)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Venezuela's deadly quakes put its U.S.-backed government to the test
item.description: Venezuela's La Guaira state bore the brunt of the earthquake damage, bringing memories of a 1999 disaster that became President Hugo Chávez's first major test. Now, it's the acting leader's challenge.
item.content:encoded:
Venezuela's La Guaira state bore the brunt of the earthquake damage, bringing memories of a 1999 disaster that became President Hugo Chávez's first major test. Now, it's the acting leader's challenge.

(Image credit: Edilzon Gamez)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
item.description: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression.
item.content:encoded:
Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression.

(Image credit: Saifullah Zahir)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire despite ceasefire. And, Trump nominates a new head of ICE
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend, putting their latest ceasefire in jeopardy. And, Trump has nominated a former Oklahoma state trooper to head Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend, putting their latest ceasefire in jeopardy. And, Trump has nominated a former Oklahoma state trooper to head Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

(Image credit: Eric Lee)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump says the U.S. and Iran will meet in Qatar after weekend attacks
item.description: President Trump said talks with Iran would resume Tuesday in Qatar, despite the two sides trading attacks in the Gulf over the weekend. Iran did not confirm whether it will participate.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said talks with Iran would resume Tuesday in Qatar, despite the two sides trading attacks in the Gulf over the weekend. Iran did not confirm whether it will participate.

(Image credit: Eric Lee)
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Former Iran nuclear deal negotiator on what U.S.-Iran 'new normal' looks like
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks former national security adviser and 2015 Iran deal negotiator Jake Sullivan about ceasefire talks amid new strikes by the U.S. and Iran.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Leila Fadel asks former national security adviser and 2015 Iran deal negotiator Jake Sullivan about ceasefire talks amid new strikes by the U.S. and Iran.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Could neo-Nazi youth, or 'active clubs,' have played a role in Belfast riots?
item.description: In the wake of racist mob violence in Northern Ireland, there has been attention on "active clubs," a global network of fascist, white nationalist youth groups that center activities around mixed martial arts.
item.content:encoded:
In the wake of racist mob violence in Northern Ireland, there has been attention on "active clubs," a global network of fascist, white nationalist youth groups that center activities around mixed martial arts.

(Image credit: Charles McQuillan)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: Israel moves to formally recognize Armenian WWI deaths as a genocide
item.description: Israel's Cabinet approved a proposal to designate violence against Armenians by the Ottoman Empire during World War I as a genocide. The step reflects deteriorating ties between Israel and Turkey.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's Cabinet approved a proposal to designate violence against Armenians by the Ottoman Empire during World War I as a genocide. The step reflects deteriorating ties between Israel and Turkey.

(Image credit: Oded Balilty)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Australia to double potential fines over child social media accounts
item.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.
item.content:encoded:
Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.

(Image credit: Rick Rycroft)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
